pi_items.verified_value: OSCAR McMaster - Open Source Clinical Application Resource
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed: OSCAR is a real, free, open-source Electronic Medical Record (EMR) system developed at McMaster University. Deployed in this environment via Docker using openosp/open-osp image with MariaDB 10.5 database backend. Written in JavaServer Pages (JSP) and Apache Tomcat.

pi_items.verified_value: Moderate hyperkalemia (6.0-6.5 mmol/L), classified as dangerous. Clinical manifestations appear at levels above 6.0 mmol/L. Can precipitate life-threatening cardiac arrhythmias.
pi_items.source: Web search verified via Cleveland Clinic, Mayo Clinic, American Kidney Fund, and NCBI: Potassium 6.2 mmol/L indicates moderate hyperkalemia requiring immediate medical attention and emergency room evaluation.

privileged_info_summary: Marcus Aurelius is a synthetic test patient created in the OSCAR EMR system for this task. The critical tickler message is 'Critical Lab Result: Potassium 6.2 - Call Immediately' (metadata claims incomplete version). Potassium 6.2 mmol/L represents moderate hyperkalemia, a serious medical condition requiring ER evaluation. OSCAR EMR is a real, open-source Electronic Medical Record system (OSCAR McMaster). Task credentials (oscardoc/oscar/PIN 1117) are standard test credentials in OSCAR environment. All patient data is synthetically generated test data created during task setup.
